title: [LV] Vectorize early exit loops with multiple exits.
author: fhahn
description:
Building on top of the recent changes to introduce BranchOnTwoConds,
this patch adds support for vectorizing loops with multiple early exits,
all dominating a countable latch. The early exits must form a
dominance chain, so we can simply check which early exit has been taken
in dominance order.

Currently LoopVectorizationLegality ensures that all exits other than
the latch must be uncountable. handleUncountableEarlyExits now collects
those uncountable exits and processes each exit.

In the vector region, we compute if any exit has been taken, by taking
the OR of all early exit conditions (EarlyExitConds) and checking if there's
any active lane.

If the early exit is taken, we exit the loop and compute which early exit
has been taken. The first taken early exit is the one where its exit
condition is true in the first active lane of EarlyExitConds.

We create a chain of dispatch blocks outside the loop to check this for
the early exit blocks ordered by dominance.

Depends on https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174016 (included in PR)

tests.test_body:
declare void @init(ptr)

define i64 @two_early_exits_same_exit_with_constant_live_outs() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @multiple_uncountable_exits() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %search1

search1:                                          ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp1, label %loop.end, label %search2

search2:                                          ; preds = %search1
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld1, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.end, label %loop.inc

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %search2
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %search1, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %search2, %search1
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %search1 ], [ 100, %search2 ], [ 43, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @early_exit_with_live_in_condition(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %p = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %inc, %latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  br i1 %cond, label %loop.end, label %search

search:                                           ; preds = %loop
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i8 %ld, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.end, label %latch

latch:                                            ; preds = %search
  %inc = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp eq i64 %inc, 1024
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %latch, %search, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ 0, %loop ], [ 1, %search ], [ 2, %latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_same_exit() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @four_early_exits_same_exit() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %C = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %C, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %gep.C = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %C, i64 %iv
  %ld.C = load i8, ptr %gep.C, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld.B, %ld.C
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %early.exit.2

early.exit.2:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %cmp4 = icmp ugt i8 %ld.C, 100
  br i1 %cmp4, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.2
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.2, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 200, %early.exit.1 ], [ 300, %early.exit.2 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_with_live_out_values() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %sum = add i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %sum, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ 99, %loop.latch ]
  %retval2 = phi i8 [ 0, %loop.header ], [ %sum, %early.exit.0 ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %ext = zext i8 %retval2 to i64
  %ret = add i64 %retval, %ext
  ret i64 %ret
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @three_early_exits_iv_and_load_live_out() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %cmp3 = icmp ugt i8 %ld.B, 100
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval.iv = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ %iv, %early.exit.1 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  %retval.ld = phi i8 [ %ld.A, %loop.header ], [ %ld.A, %early.exit.0 ], [ %ld.B, %early.exit.1 ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %ext = zext i8 %retval.ld to i64
  %ret = add i64 %retval.iv, %ext
  ret i64 %ret
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @three_early_exits_three_exit_blocks() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit1, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit2, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %cmp3 = icmp ugt i8 %ld.B, 100
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit3, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 99
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit1:                                            ; preds = %loop.header
  %ret1 = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ]
  ret i64 %ret1

exit2:                                            ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %ret2 = phi i64 [ 100, %early.exit.0 ]
  ret i64 %ret2

exit3:                                            ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %ret3 = phi i64 [ 200, %early.exit.1 ]
  ret i64 %ret3

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch
  ret i64 43
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_iv_live_out() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define { i64, i8 } @four_early_exits_with_conditional_loads() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %C = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %D = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %C, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %D, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 10
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.B, 20
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %gep.C = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %C, i64 %iv
  %ld.C = load i8, ptr %gep.C, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld.C, 30
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %early.exit.2

early.exit.2:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %gep.D = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %D, i64 %iv
  %ld.D = load i8, ptr %gep.D, align 1
  %cmp4 = icmp eq i8 %ld.D, 40
  br i1 %cmp4, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.2
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.2, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval.iv = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ %iv, %early.exit.1 ], [ %iv, %early.exit.2 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  %retval.val = phi i8 [ %ld.A, %loop.header ], [ %ld.B, %early.exit.0 ], [ %ld.C, %early.exit.1 ], [ %ld.D, %early.exit.2 ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %r1 = insertvalue { i64, i8 } undef, i64 %retval.iv, 0
  %r2 = insertvalue { i64, i8 } %r1, i8 %retval.val, 1
  ret { i64, i8 } %r2
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @three_early_exits_same_exit() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %cmp3 = icmp ugt i8 %ld.B, 100
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 200, %early.exit.1 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_iv_diff_incoming() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %iv.plus1 = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv.plus1, %early.exit.0 ], [ 200, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @three_early_exits_loads_in_different_blocks() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %C = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %C, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %gep.C = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %C, i64 %iv
  %ld.C = load i8, ptr %gep.C, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld.B, %ld.C
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ %iv, %early.exit.1 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_different_exits() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit1, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit2, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit1:                                            ; preds = %loop.header
  %ret1 = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ]
  ret i64 %ret1

exit2:                                            ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %ret2 = phi i64 [ 100, %early.exit.0 ]
  ret i64 %ret2

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch
  ret i64 43
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define { i64, i64 } @three_early_exits_multiple_live_outs() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %sum = add i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %sum, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %early.exit.1

early.exit.1:                                     ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %diff = sub i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  %cmp3 = icmp ugt i8 %diff, 100
  br i1 %cmp3, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.1
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.1, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval.iv = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ %iv, %early.exit.1 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  %retval.val = phi i8 [ %ld.A, %loop.header ], [ %sum, %early.exit.0 ], [ %diff, %early.exit.1 ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %ext = zext i8 %retval.val to i64
  %r1 = insertvalue { i64, i64 } undef, i64 %retval.iv, 0
  %r2 = insertvalue { i64, i64 } %r1, i64 %ext, 1
  ret { i64, i64 } %r2
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_load_in_early_exit_block() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ %iv, %early.exit.0 ], [ 128, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @two_early_exits_negated_condition() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp ne i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp1, label %early.exit.0, label %exit

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %cmp2 = icmp uge i8 %ld.A, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.latch, label %exit

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 128
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @multi_exit_load_in_nonexiting_block(ptr %src) {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep1, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %loop.end, label %middle.block

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.src = getelementptr inbounds i64, ptr %src, i64 %index
  %ld.out = load i64, ptr %gep.src, align 4
  br label %early.exit.2

early.exit.2:                                     ; preds = %middle.block
  %gep2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep2, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.2
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.2, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %loop.header ], [ %ld.out, %early.exit.2 ], [ 67, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @uncountable_exit_infinite_loop() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %loop ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

comments.author: androm3da
comments.body:
I'm moving my comment from the commit over here to the PR, gives better context I think.

I see this failure from libstdc++ with `_GLIBCXX_DEBUG` enabled and I suspect it came from the commit from this PR.

```
 In function:                                                                                         
      void std::sort(_RandomAccessIterator, _RandomAccessIterator, _Compare)                           
      [_RandomAccessIterator = EarlyExitInfo *, _Compare = (lambda at                                  
      /local/mnt/workspace/bcain-20260213_180548/llvm-project/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTrans 
  forms.c                                                                                              
  pp:4082:21)]                                                                                         
                                                                                                       
  Error: comparison doesn't meet irreflexive requirements, assert(!(a < a)).                           
                                                                                                       
  Objects involved in the operation:                                                                   
      instance "functor" @ 0x7ffc58648430 {                                                            
      }                                                                                                
      iterator::value_type "ordered type"  {                                                           
      }                  
```

Here's a possible fix to make the comparator pass the irreflexive requirement?

```
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.cpp                                                    
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.cpp
@@ -4080,7 +4080,8 @@ void VPlanTransforms::handleUncountableEarlyExits(VPlan &Plan,                       
   assert(!Exits.empty() && "must have at least one early exit");                                          
   // Sort exits by dominance to get the correct program order.                                            
   llvm::sort(Exits, [&VPDT](const EarlyExitInfo &A, const EarlyExitInfo &B) {                             
-    return VPDT.dominates(A.EarlyExitingVPBB, B.EarlyExitingVPBB);                                        
+    return A.EarlyExitingVPBB != B.EarlyExitingVPBB &&                                                    
+           VPDT.dominates(A.EarlyExitingVPBB, B.EarlyExitingVPBB);                                        
   });                                                                                                     

```

... I'll follow up w/a reproducer when my batch run is over ~tonight or tomorrow.

comments.author: lukel97
comments.body:
> I'm moving my comment from the commit over here to the PR, gives better context I think.
> 
> I see this failure from libstdc++ with `_GLIBCXX_DEBUG` enabled and I suspect it came from the commit from this PR.
> 
> ```
>  In function:                                                                                         
>       void std::sort(_RandomAccessIterator, _RandomAccessIterator, _Compare)                           
>       [_RandomAccessIterator = EarlyExitInfo *, _Compare = (lambda at                                  
>       /local/mnt/workspace/bcain-20260213_180548/llvm-project/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTrans 
>   forms.c                                                                                              
>   pp:4082:21)]                                                                                         
>                                                                                                        
>   Error: comparison doesn't meet irreflexive requirements, assert(!(a < a)).                           
>                                                                                                        
>   Objects involved in the operation:                                                                   
>       instance "functor" @ 0x7ffc58648430 {                                                            
>       }                                                                                                
>       iterator::value_type "ordered type"  {                                                           
>       }                  
> ```
> 
> Here's a possible fix to make the comparator pass the irreflexive requirement?
> 
> ```
> --- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.cpp                                                    
> +++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.cpp
> @@ -4080,7 +4080,8 @@ void VPlanTransforms::handleUncountableEarlyExits(VPlan &Plan,                       
>    assert(!Exits.empty() && "must have at least one early exit");                                          
>    // Sort exits by dominance to get the correct program order.                                            
>    llvm::sort(Exits, [&VPDT](const EarlyExitInfo &A, const EarlyExitInfo &B) {                             
> -    return VPDT.dominates(A.EarlyExitingVPBB, B.EarlyExitingVPBB);                                        
> +    return A.EarlyExitingVPBB != B.EarlyExitingVPBB &&                                                    
> +           VPDT.dominates(A.EarlyExitingVPBB, B.EarlyExitingVPBB);                                        
>    });                                                                                                     
> ```
> 
> ... I'll follow up w/a reproducer when my batch run is over ~tonight or tomorrow.

I think we can use VPDT.properlyDominates(A.EarlyExitingVPBB, B.EarlyExitingVPBB) too to meet the irreflexive requirement
